hero.headline: Governed human-agent engineering, rooted in the repository.
hero.body: ARAH Harness transforms your repository into the operational source of truth for agents, skills, policies, validation and human authority.

shift.headline: Software engineering is becoming human-agent engineering
shift.body: The work is no longer just writing code. It is coordinating humans, agents, models, tools, policies, context, validation and persistent memory — as one system of engineering.
shift.quote: The future of software engineering is not autonomous. It is coordinated.

problem.headline: Code generation is not the hardest problem
problem.body: The harder problem is coordinating intelligence without losing context, authority and accountability.

items.title: Agents without organizational context
items.description: Models read code locally but do not know responsibilities, architecture, domain boundaries or decision criteria.

items.title: Ephemeral prompts
items.description: Knowledge produced in AI sessions disappears when the conversation ends or the tool changes.

items.title: Autonomy without accountability
items.description: Agents execute changes with no explicit authority, scope, approval path or evidence.

items.title: Knowledge locked to vendors
items.description: Context and workflows get trapped inside copilots, proprietary platforms and specific runtimes.

items.title: Policies that are only documents
items.description: Rules live in wikis and PDFs, with no executable form an agent is forced to obey.

items.title: Uncoordinated multi-agent activity
items.description: Multiple agents act on the same repository without roles, boundaries or shared signals.

items.title: Absence of evidence
items.description: Changes land without artifacts proving what was validated, by whom, under which policy.

items.title: Context lost between sessions
items.description: Every new session starts from zero: decisions, constraints and history must be re-explained.

inversion.headline: The repository governs the agent.
inversion.body: The agent does not receive unrestricted authority. The repository provides its role, context, scope, capabilities, policies, validations and approval paths.

whatIs.headline: One operational layer, installed in your repository
whatIs.body: ARAH Harness is a repo-first harness for governing, coordinating and evolving human-agent software engineering. It installs and maintains a versioned operational layer inside the repository. Select a component to inspect it.

layersSection.headline: A layer of authority between environments and the repository
layersSection.body: Select a layer to inspect what lives there. Full detail on the Architecture page.

items.name: Repository-first
items.description: The repository is the operational source of truth.

items.name: Human in command
items.description: Accountability and merge authority remain human.

items.name: Governed agency
items.description: Every agent has an explicit role, scope and autonomy level.

items.name: Spec before code
items.description: Relevant changes start from intent and verifiable criteria.

items.name: Context on demand
items.description: Each agent receives the context appropriate to its function.

items.name: Evidence before trust
items.description: Confidence is sustained by validations and artifacts.

items.name: Persistent intelligence
items.description: Knowledge survives sessions, tools and models.

lifecycle.headline: Twelve operations, from install to regeneration

positioning.headline: A different category, not a replacement
positioning.body: ARAH can govern how these tools participate in the repository. It does not need to replace them.

notList.headline: What ARAH is not
notList.body: ARAH is a repo-first layer of governance and coordination for engineering between humans and agents. It is not:
notList.quote: Autonomy without governance does not scale.
items: A promise of software development without humans

status.headline: What exists today, what is coming
status.body: The site distinguishes shipped capability from vision. So does the product.

quickstart.headline: Install it in an existing repository
quickstart.body: Three commands to install the kernel, then a small CLI surface to discover, sync, validate and evolve. Full guide in the documentation.

cta.headline: Agents propose. Humans select. The repository remembers.
cta.body: Your engineering context should belong to your repository, not to a model provider.

parts.name: Agent manifests
parts.definition: Versioned contracts declaring each agent: role, scope, allowed paths, skills, autonomy level and escalation rules.
parts.role: Defines who an agent is and what it may touch before any model is invoked.
parts.maturity: Available — validated by executable gates on every change.
parts.example:
# .agents/backend-payments.yaml
role: operational
domains: [payments]
paths: ["backend/payments/**"]
skills: [refactor, test-first]
autonomy: propose-only

parts.name: Skills
parts.definition: Deterministic, executable procedures an agent is authorized to run — not free-form prompts.
parts.role: Turns "how we do things here" into versioned, testable capability units.
parts.example:
# .skills/test-first/skill.yaml
name: test-first
inputs: [spec]
steps:
  - generate failing test
  - implement
  - run gates

parts.name: Domains
parts.definition: Explicit areas of the repository with responsibilities, paths, references and validations.
parts.role: Gives agents the organizational map humans carry in their heads.
parts.maturity: Available — proposed by discovery, synced via domain sync.
parts.example:
payments:
  paths: ["backend/payments/**"]
  owners: [platform-team]
  advisors: [payments-specialist]
  gates: [spec, security]

parts.name: Specialists
parts.definition: Consultative agents bound to a domain, consulted by operational agents before changes.
parts.role: Encodes domain judgment: constraints, invariants, decision criteria.
parts.example:
# consulted automatically when
# choreography matches the domain
arah organism signal \
  --type consultation.request \
  --domain payments

parts.name: Path-based choreography
parts.definition: Selection of agents and skills based on the files and areas affected by a change.
parts.role: Routes work: a diff in backend/payments/** activates payment agents, their skills and gates.
parts.example:
match: "backend/payments/**"
activate:
  agents: [backend-payments]
  advisors: [payments-specialist]
  gates: [spec, security, build]

parts.name: Autonomy policies
parts.definition: Declared autonomy levels per agent and per path: what may be proposed, executed or must escalate.
parts.role: Replaces implicit trust with explicit, versioned authority.
parts.maturity: Available — enforced at choreography and gate time.
parts.example:
autonomy:
  default: propose-only
  overrides:
    - paths: ["docs/**"]
      level: execute-with-audit

parts.name: Executable gates
parts.definition: Validations that run as code: manifests, specs, docs, graph, security, dependencies, build.
parts.role: Makes policy enforceable — a change that fails a gate does not reach a pull request.
parts.maturity: Available — runs locally and in CI.
parts.example:
$ arah sync-check
✓ manifests valid
✓ specs satisfied
✓ graph consistent
✗ security: secret in diff
gate failed → change blocked

parts.name: Signal bus
parts.definition: Persistent, typed, append-only events for communication between agents and cells.
parts.role: Lets agents coordinate across sessions and tools without sharing a chat window.

parts.name: Audit ledger
parts.definition: Append-only record of actions, results, autonomy decisions and session events.
parts.role: Answers: what happened, by which agent, under which policy, with what evidence.
parts.example:
2026-07-18 14:03 agent=backend-payments
action=skill.test-first result=pass
autonomy=propose-only gates=4/4
evidence=.arah/audit/run-8f31/

parts.name: Agent graph
parts.definition: Auditable representation of relations between agents, skills, domains and responsibilities.
parts.role: Makes the whole system inspectable — and validatable as a gate.
parts.example:
$ arah export-graph
nodes: 18 agents · 23 skills · 6 domains
edges: 61
→ docs/_meta/graph.json

parts.name: Discovery
parts.definition: Inspection of stack, frameworks, structure and directories to propose domains and specialists.
parts.role: Bootstraps governance from what the repository already is.
parts.example:
$ arah discover
stack: node20 + pnpm + nest
candidates: payments, catalog, infra
→ docs/_meta/discovery.proposed.yaml

parts.name: Evolution
parts.definition: Analysis of ledger, signals and telemetry to propose system improvements — always under human selection.
parts.role: The harness improves itself the same way code changes: by reviewed proposals.
parts.example:
$ arah evolve
proposal: split "backend" domain
evidence: 78% of signals scoped to payments
→ PR #142 (awaiting human review)

parts.name: Regeneration
parts.definition: Updates the kernel, re-runs discovery, syncs domains, regenerates graphs and validates the installation.
parts.role: Homeostasis: keeps the installed harness consistent as the repository evolves.
parts.example:
$ arah regenerate --update-kernel
kernel 0.3.1 → 0.4.1
discovery re-run · domains synced
graph regenerated · install valid

layers.name: Human Intent
layers.detail: Where authority originates and returns. Humans set goals, decide direction, review proposals and hold merge authority.
layers.itemsLong: goals · architectural decisions · reviews · approval · merge

layers.name: AI Environments
layers.detail: The tools where models run. ARAH does not replace them — it governs how they participate in the repository.
layers.itemsLong: Claude Code · Cursor · GitHub Copilot · Codex · local models · custom agents

layers.name: ARAH Harness
layers.detail: The governance and coordination layer: versioned contracts that define who may act, where, with which capabilities, under which validation.
layers.itemsLong: agents · skills · choreography · policies · gates · signals · graph · audit · evolution

layers.name: Repository
layers.detail: The operational source of truth: everything the harness knows and enforces lives here, versioned and portable.
layers.itemsLong: code · architecture · domain · specifications · documentation · tests · infrastructure · CI

layers.name: Delivery
layers.detail: Changes only reach production through the human path: pull request, review, merge, deployment.

areas.name: Discover
areas.description: The harness observes what the repository already is before proposing structure.

areas.name: Model
areas.description: What was discovered becomes explicit, versioned contracts.

areas.name: Coordinate
areas.description: Work is routed to the right agents and capabilities by the paths it touches.

areas.name: Execute
areas.description: Agents act through deterministic, authorized procedures.

areas.name: Validate
areas.description: Every relevant change passes executable gates before reaching review.

areas.name: Observe
areas.description: Everything that happens is recorded and inspectable.

areas.name: Govern
areas.description: Authority is explicit, and merge remains human.

areas.name: Evolve
areas.description: The system proposes improvements from evidence; humans select.
